perturbation.original_text: As used herein "Confidential Information" means all proprietary or confidential information of the Disclosing Party, including but not limited to Confidential Information regarding its products, suppliers, customers, technology, pricing, business plan, activities or know-how, whether disclosed directly or indirectly, in writing (marked as Confidential Information), orally or by drawings or inspection of documents.
perturbation.changed_text: As used herein "Confidential Information" means all proprietary or confidential information of the Disclosing Party, only if provided in a written format (marked as Confidential Information). Confidential Information explicitly excludes all other proprietary or confidential information of the Disclosing Party, including but not limited to Confidential Information regarding its products, suppliers, customers, technology, pricing, business plan, activities or know-how, if disclosed orally or by drawings or inspection of documents.
perturbation.explanation: The change restricts the definition of "Confidential Information" to only written information. This directly contradicts later clauses referencing other forms of disclosure such as: 'orally or by drawings or inspection of documents.' This creates an internal contradiction because some sections assume that verbal information or information shared visually also qualifies as confidential, while the definition explicitly excludes those formats.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used herein "Confidential Information" means all proprietary or confidential information of the Disclosing Party, only if provided in a written format (marked as Confidential Information). Confidential Information explicitly excludes all other proprietary or confidential information of the Disclosing Party, including but not limited to Confidential Information regarding its products, suppliers, customers, technology, pricing, business plan, activities or know-how, if disclosed orally or by drawings or inspection of documents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly restricts 'Confidential Information' to only written formats, directly contradicting the original definition which included oral disclosures and information shared through drawings or inspections. Since the changed and contradicted texts are the same, the contradiction is evident. The explanation indicates that later clauses might reference these other forms of disclosure, creating an inconsistency within the document regarding what qualifies as confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party shall, without the other party's prior written consent (a) disclose to any person or entity (other than the persons employed by either party who are actively and directly participating in the Stated Purpose) any information about the parties' discussions regarding the Stated Purpose or the terms, conditions or other facts relating thereto including the fact that discussions are taking place with respect thereto or the status thereof, or the fact that the Confidential Information has been made available to either party, except to the extent that such disclosure is required by applicable laws, rules or regulations or government requirement or court order (provided, however, that the Recipient shall promptly advise the Disclosing Party of its notice of any such requirement or order); or (b) use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Stated Purpose, without limiting the foregoing, the Recipient shall not trade in the securities of the Disclosing Party while in the possession of Confidential Information relating to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may, without the other party's prior written consent (a) disclose to any person or entity (other than the persons employed by either party who are actively and directly participating in the Stated Purpose) any information about the parties' discussions regarding the Stated Purpose or the terms, conditions or other facts relating thereto including the fact that discussions are taking place with respect thereto or the status thereof, or the fact that the Confidential Information has been made available to either party, except to the extent that such disclosure is required by applicable laws, rules or regulations or government requirement or court order (provided, however, that the Recipient shall promptly advise the Disclosing Party of its notice of any such requirement or order); or (b) use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Stated Purpose, without limiting the foregoing, the Recipient shall not trade in the securities of the Disclosing Party while in the possession of Confidential Information relating to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Neither party shall" to "Either party may" disclose information without consent, the modified text directly contradicts the fundamental purpose of a non-disclosure agreement, which is to prevent unauthorized disclosure of confidential information. This creates a contradiction, because it makes the agreement to be not confidential anymore, so it contradict the title of the document, it is a Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement, so it would be expected to not have the power of disclosing confidential information. This fundamentally undermines the agreement's core principle.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PIERCING PAGODA, INC. 3910 Adler Place Post Office Box 25007 Lehigh Valley, Pa 18002 MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This Agreement, made as of March 28, 2000 (the Effective Date), by and between Piercing Pagoda, Inc., a Delaware corporation (including its affiliates and their respective successors, transferees and permitted assigns, "PPI"), and the undersigned (including its affiliates and their respective successors, transferees and permitted assigns, "Company") in connection with, our consideration of a possible business transaction (the "Stated Purpose") each party may disclose certain of its proprietary and confidential information to the other party in order to facilitate discussion and analyses.
perturbation.justification: The original text states, "Neither party shall, without the other party's prior written consent (a) disclose to any person or entity... any information..." The changed text modifies this to "Either party may, without the other party's prior written consent (a) disclose to any person or entity... any information..." This creates a direct contradiction. The contradicted text is the title of the document, "MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT". A non-disclosure agreement fundamentally implies that confidential information should not be disclosed. The change allows either party to disclose information without consent, directly conflicting with the core purpose and title of the agreement. This fits the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' because it creates ambiguity with the obligations stated in the title.

perturbation.original_text: At the request of the Disclosing Party, the Recipient shall promptly return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in whatever medium, including any and all copies thereof. Such return (or, if authorized, destruction of Confidential Information) shall be confirmed in writing by the Recipient to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: At the request of the Disclosing Party, the Recipient is encouraged but not obligated to return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in whatever medium, including any and all copies thereof. The return or destruction of Confidential Information does not require written confirmation by the Recipient to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall promptly return' to 'is encouraged but not obligated to return' and removing the requirement for written confirmation introduces ambiguity. This contradicts the initial statements about the recipient being responsible for keeping confidentiality of the information, because there is no longer a clear requirement to return or destroy confidential information upon request, which implies that the recipient can indefinitely retain the confidential information, contradicting all previous text about responsibility and confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient shall take responsible steps to ensure that all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is kept confidential; provided, however, that such information may be disclosed to those employees or agents of the Recipient who have a need to know such information, or to such other party as may be agreed to by the Disclosing Party in writing, only if each such employee or party is informed by the Recipient of the confidential and proprietary nature of such information and of the confidentiality undertakings of the Recipient contained herein. The Recipient shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its employees or agents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the obligation to return confidential information and removes the requirement for written confirmation. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that 'The Recipient shall take responsible steps to ensure that all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is kept confidential'. The original text had a strong obligation to return the information upon request which helped ensure confidentiality. By making the return optional and removing confirmation, the changed text undermines the recipient's responsibility to maintain confidentiality, thus creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate the other party's damages from any breach of the covenants set forth in this Agreement. Accordingly, the parties agree that if either party breaches or threatens to breach any of such covenants, the non-breaching party will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specific performance of any such provision of this Agreement. The parties further agree that no bond or other security shall be required in obtaining such equitable relief and each party hereby consents to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that it would be possible to measure and calculate the other party's damages from any breach of the covenants set forth in this Agreement. Accordingly, the parties agree that if either party breaches or threatens to breach any of such covenants, the non-breaching party will waive the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach. The parties further agree that a bond or other security shall be required in obtaining equitable relief.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'impossible or inadequate to measure' to 'possible to measure' damages and waiving the right to an injunction directly contradicts the intent of the original clause, which aimed to secure equitable relief (injunction) because monetary damages were deemed insufficient. The waiver of injunctive relief and the requirement of a bond undermine the protection afforded to the Disclosing Party, by being contradictory to the 'The Recipient shall take responsible steps to ensure that all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is kept confidential' clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient shall take responsible steps to ensure that all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is kept confidential; provided, however, that such information may be disclosed to those employees or agents of the Recipient who have a need to know such information, or to such other party as may be agreed to by the Disclosing Party in writing, only if each such employee or party is informed by the Recipient of the confidential and proprietary nature of such information and of the confidentiality undertakings of the Recipient contained herein. The Recipient shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its employees or agents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the original agreement by stating damages are measurable and waiving the right to an injunction while also requiring a bond. This contradicts the 'Recipient shall take responsible steps to ensure that all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is kept confidential' clause because waiving injunctive relief and requiring a bond undermines the protection afforded to the Disclosing Party, making it more difficult to enforce confidentiality, thus representing a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that the Disclosing Party is not making any representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information furnished by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party (except to the extent and only to such effect as shall be expressly set forth in an executed and delivered definitive agreement between the parties to effect the Stated Purpose (the "Definitive Agreement")). Neither the Disclosing Party, its affiliates nor any of their respective officers, directors, employees or agents shall have any liability to the Recipient or any of its Representatives relating to or arising from the use of the Confidential Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that the Disclosing Party warrants the accuracy and completeness of all Confidential Information furnished by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party. The Disclosing Party, its affiliates and their respective officers, directors, employees or agents shall have full liability to the Recipient or any of its Representatives relating to or arising from the use of the Confidential Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the language to explicitly state that the Disclosing Party warrants accuracy and completeness and accepts full liability, the modified text contradicts the intent to limit liability. Because this change directly contradicts a general disclaimer and creates new liabilities, it creates an internal ambiguity about which section is followed or given precedence. Also, it contradicts the 1st point of the contract 'The Recipient shall take responsible steps to ensure that all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is kept confidential' because now the disclosing party is liable
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient shall take responsible steps to ensure that all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is kept confidential; provided, however, that such information may be disclosed to those employees or agents of the Recipient who have a need to know such information, or to such other party as may be agreed to by the Disclosing Party in writing, only if each such employee or party is informed by the Recipient of the confidential and proprietary nature of such information and of the confidentiality undertakings of the Recipient contained herein. The Recipient shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its employees or agents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Disclosing Party warrants the accuracy and completeness of all confidential information and accepts full liability. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'The Recipient shall take responsible steps to ensure that all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is kept confidential'. By shifting responsibility for the accuracy and confidentiality of the information to the Disclosing Party, the change contradicts the Recipient's obligation to keep the information confidential, creating a strong in-text contradiction as described in the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.
